prompt: I need to switch from the feature/payments branch to main, work on a hotfix, and get back to my payments work without losing context. Set up the context switch for me.
I'm working in the production AWS account and need to quickly jump to the staging account to check a config, then come back. What state do I need to save and what's the fastest way to switch?
I need to switch between our monorepo's frontend and backend workspaces rapidly. Each has its own node_modules, env vars, and running dev server. Help me set up quick context switching.

I'm debugging in the Python REPL with a bunch of state loaded. I need to quickly check something in the Node.js project next door and come back. What's the cleanest way to context-switch without losing my REPL state?
I'm SSHed into server-a and need to quickly check a log on server-b, then come back to server-a exactly where I was. Set up the jump.

I have two terminal sessions — one tailing production logs and one running a local dev server. I need to switch between them quickly while keeping both alive. Help me set up tmux panes or sessions for this.

I need to switch my local Docker environment from the 'analytics' compose stack to the 'payments' compose stack. Both use different services and env files. What's the fastest way to swap and then swap back?
I'm working in our US-East-1 Kubernetes context and need to quickly check pod status in EU-West-1, then switch back. Help me context-switch between kubectl clusters cleanly.
I'm mid-task on the wizards-of-the-ghosts project and need to quickly jump to another project to review a PR, then come back. What context should I bookmark before switching?

I need to switch between two different Python virtual environments (one for the Django app, one for the data pipeline) while keeping my current shell history and working directory context. Set up the toggle.
